---
title: 'Romans 8: In God We Trust!?'
date: '2014-08-13T03:44:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4097076063664802135'
blogger_filename: '/2014/08/romans-8-in-god-we-trust.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<br /><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/in-god-we-trust-by-aaron-van-dorn-a84d1b3d6a94.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="&quot;In god we trust&quot; by Aaron van Dorn Partial photo of a quarter, emphasising the phrase &quot;In god we trust&quot;." border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/in-god-we-trust-by-aaron-van-dorn-a84d1b3d6a94.jpg" height="270" title="&quot;In god we trust&quot; by Aaron van Dorn Partial photo of a quarter, emphasising the phrase &quot;In god we trust&quot;." width="400" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">"In god we trust" by <a href="https://www.flickr.com/photos/aaronvandorn/9396598120/in/photolist-fjm2fq-cQSS19-djitRS-6iX7zd-8NvRJX-9hABMh-ksqbPH-5DwcwR-5DAuVo-5DwcCa-fKNN29-cFrCB-u2aQQ-oFkWyo-4vqZR-5Dwczg-nSeorV-4n6wj4-9SyFY7-5qD3KC-5Dwcxt-5DwcAz-5DAuYS-5DAuYw-5DAuUJ-5DAuUf-5DG6Hs-6QEwBq-8dpJbv-9kPcNX-9mCUyk-dPTzNj-fzcUgg-4Njz7d-4NjzdC-4Njzc3-7VSb15-2T3WwC-cQ1Wjj-csNfzU-jqYoDa-jF8HX-dR9auP-8uG3vg-4D5Xcp-8tzLdz-8tAgf8-8uGriT-8tVkcp-8tVknz" target="_blank">Aaron van Dorn</a></td></tr></tbody></table><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><br /><div><h3><a href="https://www.bible.com/users/darrellwolfe/reading-plans/28-joseph-prince?initial=true" target="_blank">Romans 8</a> is PACKED full of good stuff!&nbsp;</h3><h4></h4><h4>Who knew all this was in the same chapter!?</h4><h4></h4>Why do I find it so hard to believe God? I mean over, and over, and over, He has proven himself faithful to me, in very real and practical ways. Just recently, the favor He's shown in moving me to my new job position, has been nothing short of miraculous. <br /><br />Nevertheless, as soon as the next trial comes along, I start doubting again. After awhile, if the same problem is coming up over and over, the only place to look is in the mirror.<br /><br />ME? You mean I'm the problem? Well, yes, I am. But that's not the mirror I'm referring to.&nbsp; <br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><br /></i><a href="http://biblehub.com/esv/james/1.htm" target="_blank"><i>&nbsp;James 1</i></a><br /><i><br /></i><i>22But be doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves. 23For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man who looks intently at his natural face in a mirror. 24For he looks at himself and goes away and at once forgets what he was like. 25But the one who looks into the perfect law, the law of liberty, and perseveres, being no hearer who forgets but a doer who acts, he will be blessed in his doing. </i></blockquote><br />When I spend time in the perfect Word of God, it shows me things I couldn't see. Just as&nbsp; mirror shows my how crazy my hair really is in the first minutes of morning, the mirror of the Word shows me how crazy my thoughts are before I washed them in His Word.<br /><br />I had one such mirror moment reading Romans 8 this past week.<br /><br /><h3>-Romans 8 Study- </h3><h4>Yield to HolySpirit </h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><br /><i><span class="verse v6" data-usfm="ROM.8.6"><span class="label">6</span><span class="content">For to be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.</span></span></i><br /><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v6" data-usfm="ROM.8.6"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v7" data-usfm="ROM.8.7"><span class="label">7</span><span class="content">Because the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.</span></span></i></div><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v7" data-usfm="ROM.8.7"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v8" data-usfm="ROM.8.8"><span class="label">8</span><span class="content">So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.</span></span></i></div><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v8" data-usfm="ROM.8.8"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v9" data-usfm="ROM.8.9"><span class="label">9</span><span class="content">But  ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of  God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is  none of his.</span></span></i></div><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v9" data-usfm="ROM.8.9"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v10" data-usfm="ROM.8.10"><span class="label">10</span><span class="content">And if Christ be in you, the body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.</span></span></i></div><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v10" data-usfm="ROM.8.10"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v11" data-usfm="ROM.8.11"><span class="label">11</span><span class="content">But  if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you,  he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal  bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.</span></span></i></div></blockquote></div><div><br />I've  always used vs 11 as a healing scripture, which is just fine, nothing  wrong with that. But today I saw it in a new and fresh light. If I have a  carnal mind, I'm envoking the curse/death. If I engage the spiritual  mind (mindsets) I am envoking blessing/life. The same Spirit that raised  Jesus from the dead is in my body, quickening (making alive) my mortal  flesh.<br /><br />That INCLUDES my mind! HolySpirit is inside of my, assisting in the renewing of my mind. I don't have to strive for a new mindset, or work hard at it.<br /><br />I need only yield to what He is processing in me. <br /><br /><h4>How do I engage this spiritual mind? </h4></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div><i><br /></i><i><span class="verse v16" data-usfm="ROM.8.16"><span class="label">16</span><span class="content">The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:</span></span></i><br /><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v16" data-usfm="ROM.8.16"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v17" data-usfm="ROM.8.17"><span class="label">17</span><span class="content">And  if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if  so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.</span></span></i></div></div></blockquote><div><blockquote class="tr_bq">--</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><span class="verse v26" data-usfm="ROM.8.26"><span class="label">26</span><span class="content">Likewise  the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should  pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us  with groanings which cannot be uttered.</span></span></i><br /><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v26" data-usfm="ROM.8.26"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v27" data-usfm="ROM.8.27"><span class="label">27</span><span class="content">And  he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit,  because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of  God.</span></span></i></div></blockquote><span class="verse v27" data-usfm="ROM.8.27"><span class="content"></span></span></div><div><br /><b>HolySpirit  bears witness to my Spirit</b>, first of my adoption, and then to any truth  I need, because he is here to lead me into all truth (according to  John). Likewise, he helps me in my weakness by interceding for me and  praying out the Will of God in my life.<br /><br /> I can tap into this during my  times of prayer, meditation, and silence. <br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><span class="verse v28" data-usfm="ROM.8.28"><span class="label">28</span><span class="content">And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.</span></span></i><span class="verse v28" data-usfm="ROM.8.28"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v29" data-usfm="ROM.8.29"><span class="label"></span></span></blockquote>This  process of allowing him to pray through you, specifically through times  of silence and praying in tongues, is how he works all things together  for Good!<br /><br /><br /><h4>Believing God for provision...</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div class="p"><span class="verse v30" data-usfm="ROM.8.30"><span class="content">  </span></span><i><span class="verse v31 focused" data-usfm="ROM.8.31"><span class="label">31</span><span class="content">What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who can be against us?</span></span></i></div><div class="p"><i><span class="verse v31 focused" data-usfm="ROM.8.31"><span class="content">  </span></span><span class="verse v32" data-usfm="ROM.8.32"><span class="label">32</span><span class="content">He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?</span></span></i></div></blockquote></div>If  God is for me, WHO can be against me?<br /><br />NO ONE!<br /><br />He didn't spare His own  Son...<br /><br />So why, here on earth, would I doubt His desire to give me freely  ALL THINGS?!!?!?!?!<br /><br />Once I get that straight, and  really accept God had my best in mind, I'll be able to  freely trust Him for all things.&nbsp;<br /><br /><b>I must yield to how much he loves me! </b><br /><br /><h4>Take away's </h4><ul><li>Yield to HolySpirit's instruction/leading.</li><li>HolySpirit bears Witness to me. </li><li>Yield to the Love of God.&nbsp;</li><li><b>So it really is all about #BeStillBeLed! (<a href="http://ctt.ec/U0Uow" target="_blank">Tweet That!</a>)</b></li></ul><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;" target="_blank">About This Blog, and Darrell.&nbsp;</a><br /><br />*My apologies for posting a day late.</div><h4><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">This Weeks Resource:</span></h4><div><div><ul><li><a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00A411R1Q/ref=as_li_tl?ie=UTF8&amp;camp=1789&amp;creative=390957&amp;creativeASIN=B00A411R1Q&amp;linkCode=as2&amp;tag=your0f4d-20">Blessing Of The Lord: Makes Rich And He Adds No Sorrow With It, By Kenneth Copeland</a><img alt="" border="0" src="http://ir-na.amazon-adsystem.com/e/ir?t=your0f4d-20&amp;l=as2&amp;o=1&amp;a=B00A411R1Q" height="1" style="border: none !important; margin: 0px !important;" width="1" /> A study of "The Blessing" through the Old and New Testament, from Abraham to Jesus.&nbsp;  </li></ul></div></div><div><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16.363636016845703px;">Affiliate Disclosure:</span></i></span><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16.363636016845703px;">I earn&nbsp;commissions&nbsp;off of some of my suggestions.&nbsp;</span></i><i style="line-height: 16.363636016845703px;">Full Disclaimers (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i><br /><i>See my Store for more suggestions (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i></span><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><br /></i></span><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><br /></i></span></div><h4>And now I leave you with a question:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>Have you struggled believing God will provide natural things you want/need?</b></blockquote><br /><div><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;">To Respond Leave A Comment on This Post</span></div><br /><br /><br /><br />
